title: Minimal Armor Pack
description: Shorted emissive armor  textures for the better view on players' skins.
body:
![ ](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a75a3bb1543ed805cdc6bae25d1105291dbf8e25.png)

[![CurseForge](https://img.shields.io/curseforge/dt/1168962?style=for-the-badge&logo=CurseForge&labelColor=16181C&color=F16436)](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/texture-packs/minimal-armor-pack)
[![Linktree](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b8e05ba6076f2a09de1434258f72867397b16f74.png)](https://linktr.ee/xivill)
# Heads Up:
Choose the correct file for your Minecraft version! **From 1.21.2 onward, armor textures  are stored differently**, so this pack has separate versions for compatibility.

For emissive textures
[ETF](https://modrinth.com/mod/entitytexturefeatures) is required!

# About the Resource Pack:
This pack was created to minimize armor textures without removing them entirely. If you’re tired of the standard armor models and prefer seeing more players' skins, this pack is a perfect fit.


![ ](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/52d49ddb93b9cfe907e9bbdd927bdb58da27a607.gif)



> However, helmet textures (except for the turtle shell) have been removed, as I couldn’t think of a design that didn’t rely on overly generic ideas like turning helmets into crowns.
> I also left gold, leather, and chainmail armor untouched since these are less commonly used by players... 
> 
> <details>
> <summary>Spoiler</summary>
> 
> ...and I am lazy
> 
> </details>

## What’s Changed?
- Leggings → Belt 
- Chestplate → Buckle 
- Boots → Belt Daggers

![ ](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/91cbeced36cdc63926bb9dbe2a5c214a2f8c4413.gif)
